title: On-board Remote-Sensing Foundation Models for Unsupervised Change Detection of Disaster Events
abstract: arXiv:2606.27018v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Remote Sensing Foundation Models (RSFMs) have emerged as a powerful alternative to supervised models for Earth Observation, allowing satellites to autonomously trigger high-resolution captures or adjust tasking parameters upon detecting an anomaly, thereby maximizing the utility of the mission's limited power and computational resources. RSFMs are versatile, unified encoders that optimize onboard storage for multiple orbital applications while ensuring high-fidelity feature extraction. In particular, unsupervised change detection with RSFMs offers a well-informed and transformative path for disaster monitoring without expensive labels. In this paper, we present a novel unsupervised detection method based on ResNet (RSFM) + FPN which identifies a wide spectrum of anomalies by detecting subtle semantic shifts in the latent space between successive orbital passes. By relying on an untrained FPN architecture and its intrinsic priors, the s

translated_title: 面向灾害事件无监督变化检测的星载遥感基础模型
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27018v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：遥感基础模型（RSFMs）已成为地球观测中有监督模型的一种强大替代方案，使卫星能够在检测到异常时自主触发高分辨率捕获或调整任务参数，从而最大化任务有限功率和计算资源的效用。RSFMs是多功能、统一的编码器，可优化多个轨道应用的星载存储，同时确保高保真特征提取。特别是，基于RSFMs的无监督变化检测为灾害监测提供了一条信息丰富且具有变革性的路径，无需昂贵的标注。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于ResNet（RSFM）+ FPN的新型无监督检测方法，通过检测连续轨道通过之间潜在空间中的细微语义偏移，识别广泛的异常。该方法依赖未经训练的FPN架构及其内在先验知识。
tldr: 本文提出了一种基于ResNet和FPN的无监督变化检测方法，利用遥感基础模型在星载平台上通过检测潜在空间语义偏移来识别灾害事件中的异常，无需标注数据。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a novel unsupervised change detection method based on ResNet and FPN, leveraging remote sensing foundation models on-board to identify anomalies in disaster events by detecting semantic shifts in latent space without requiring labeled data.
